title: AirREGI Review: Free POS Features, Costs, and Limitations
metaTitle: AirREGI Review: Free POS Features, Costs, and Limitations
description: Review AirREGI's free POS scope, required hardware, inventory, customer and sales functions, AirPAY integration, and implementation caveats.
lead: AirREGI is a free POS application for iPad and iPhone. Core order entry, checkout, catalog, inventory, customer, and sales analysis functions have no software subscription fee. A production setup still requires a deliberate hardware, payment, printing, cash, and ordering design.
author: Luqevora.com Editorial Team

ctas.label: Check AirREGI's current free account terms

sources.label: Airレジ — 機能・できること

sections.heading: Verdict: a strong starting point for lowering POS software cost
body: AirREGI is attractive when a retailer, restaurant, or service business wants core checkout and sales management without an initial or recurring POS software charge.
The free software label is not the total cost. The business must model iPad or iPhone hardware, receipt printing, cash drawers, scanners, payment acceptance, and any AirREGI Order modules.

sections.heading: Pricing: the POS application is the free component
body: Official information states that AirREGI's initial fee, monthly fee, core functions, and standard support are free without a qualifying usage restriction.

Implementation cost depends on existing equipment and operating requirements. Decide whether the store needs printed receipts, cash storage, barcodes, customer displays, cash recycling, and additional ordering devices.
bullets: Provide a compatible iPad or iPhone
Add printers and cash hardware when required
AirPAY and AirREGI Order are separate services

sections.heading: Core functions and operational coverage
body: The documented feature set includes order entry, checkout, returns, product setup, cost fields, inventory, stocktaking, customer records, sales analysis, CSV exports, and multi-store management.
Connections to accounting software, AirPAY, reservation tools, and AirREGI Order allow the system to expand beyond a standalone register.

sections.heading: Advantages
body: A store can test product setup, staff training, checkout, and reporting before committing to a software subscription. Product images, barcodes, split checkout, and sales reports cover many everyday workflows.
AirPAY integration can transfer the transaction amount and reduce duplicate entry. Accounting connections can also reduce manual sales posting.

sections.heading: Limitations and risks
body: The environment is centered on compatible Apple devices, so an Android-only hardware plan will not work. Peripheral compatibility, printing, cash control, and synchronization after connectivity loss require testing.
Restaurants that need handheld ordering, kitchen workflows, or mobile ordering must price AirREGI Order separately instead of treating every ordering function as part of the free POS application.

sections.heading: Pre-launch checklist
body: Run a realistic test covering product setup, discounts, returns, split checkout, closing, inventory adjustments, staff permissions, and data export. Verify every required accounting or payment integration and its synchronization timing.

Before rollout, document who owns menu maintenance, tax settings, discounts, refunds, closing procedures, device updates, and staff training. A free application can reduce software cost, but operational quality still depends on hardware readiness, clear permissions, and a tested fallback when the tablet, printer, network, or connected payment service is unavailable.
bullets: Required number of tablets, printers, and cash drawers

faqs.question: Can Airレジ be used for free?
faqs.answer: The core AirREGI POS application and standard support are free, while hardware, payments, and ordering modules are separate.

faqs.question: What should be tested before launch?
faqs.answer: Reproduce a full trading day with real products, checkout, refunds, closing, connectivity loss, staff permissions, and data export.

faqs.question: Which cost matters most in a comparison?
faqs.answer: Compare a three-year total covering processing, devices, subscriptions, connectivity, peripherals, settlement, and support.
